subagent_reports.topic: Orchestrator operational-delegation: discovery prior-research extension + opportunity scout

sources.label: Local proposal/problem (adv_change_show)
sources.summary: Confirms scope: prose in adv.md, table in ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md/adv-apply.md, mirror to adv-atc, new rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01. 7-day audit: primary adv ~82% cost, ~10.8k reads, ~7.2k shell/test calls outside task graph.

sources.label: Spec rq-contextShed02 (advance-meta/spec.json:962-1015)
sources.summary: Existing law: adv.md Context-Optimal Execution MUST be prose bullets (NO table / no | pipes), reference 4-question AND test + floor; tables live on ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md/adv-apply.md. Scenario rq-contextShed02.3 says drift tests enforce this.

sources.label: delegation-defaults spec matrix
sources.summary: Canonical per-step delegation matrix is task/gate-scoped only (proposal..reflect). No orchestrator-session operational-work entry. Confirms the gap the change targets.

sources.label: Table surfaces (ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md:722-738, adv-apply.md:409-434)
sources.summary: Existing per-task Delegation Routing tables (priority 1..5 incl. 4.5 context-shed). New orchestrator-ops table must be a distinct, clearly-labeled second table, not edits to these.

sources.label: adv-atc.md (.opencode/agents/adv-atc.md)
sources.summary: adv-atc has NO Context-Optimal Execution / Sub-Agent Policy / context-shed prose section at all — it does not currently mirror adv.md's existing shed prose. 'Mirror the same prose' is net-new content, not a copy.

sources.label: Drift-test audit (grep across plugin/src/**/*.test.ts)
sources.summary: NO existing test asserts rq-contextShed02 tokens or no-pipe on adv.md. handoff-footer-drift.test.ts checks adv.md Output Contract only. delegation-matrix.test.ts checks the spec matrix, not agent-file prose. The 'drift tests forbid table pipes there' claim in the proposal is aspirational, not currently implemented.

sources.label: Exa: multi-agent delegation best practice 2026
sources.locator: workos.com/blog/ai-agent-delegation-multi-agent-security; dev.to amitkayal practical delegation pattern; agility-at-scale.com multi-agent-systems; zylos.ai 2026-03-08 delegation patterns
sources.summary: Canonical pattern: orchestrator = coordinator owning understanding/decisions/judgment/review; sheds retrieval/analysis/execution/verify to limited-tool limited-context specialists. Google ADK, Anthropic, AgentOrchestra, MIT Manager-Agent. Keep authority on primary; workers get less scope. Matches proposal exactly.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Proposal shape is architecturally sound and matches both ADV's existing internal pattern (task-level context-shed split: prose in adv.md per rq-contextShed02 + table on instruction surfaces per rq-contextShed01) and external canonical multi-agent practice (coordinator owns decisions, sheds operational work to scoped workers). The boring/proven choice is exactly what is proposed: additive instruction+spec layer, no runtime enforcement, consistent with ADV's already self-enforced sub-agent policy. Two material discovery findings extend the prior research: (1) the proposal's premise that 'drift tests forbid table pipes' in adv.md is NOT currently true — no such test exists; the new requirement should ADD the missing drift assertions (prose tokens + no-pipe on adv.md, operational-trigger table token incl. GitHub-CI on the table surfaces) rather than assume they already guard the surface. (2) adv-atc.md currently lacks ANY context-optimal/shed prose, so 'mirror to adv-atc' is net-new authoring + needs its own drift coverage, and must respect adv-atc's question:false constraint. No external-solution adoption needed — this is a prompt/spec authoring task, not a build-vs-buy decision.

validation.notes: No blockers. Two scope clarifications for design: codify NEW drift assertions (don't assume existing), and treat adv-atc mirror as net-new prose with its own test. Honor rq-contextShed02 prose-only invariant: new adv.md operational-delegation content must also be prose bullets (no | pipes) to avoid violating the very law it sits beside.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed. In design: (a) add rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01 to advance-meta as additive, defining authority-KEEP set + operational SHED triggers (incl. GitHub CI/check-run investigation) + the 'no second recon/shell/test/CI cycle before delegating' rule; (b) require NEW drift tests (adv.md+adv-atc.md operational-shed prose tokens with zero table pipes in that section; ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md+adv-apply.md operational trigger table containing the GitHub-CI token) — do not rely on non-existent existing guards; (c) keep new instruction-surface table clearly labeled as orchestrator-session operational routing, distinct from per-task Step 4.5; (d) explicitly out-of-scope: runtime enforcement, OMR model-routing config, task-level 4.5 semantics.
follow_ups: Design must decide canonical worker mapping for ops triggers (explore vs adv-tron vs general) and keep it consistent across adv.md prose and the instruction-surface table.

Open user-facing question for design: should the operational-shed table live in BOTH ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md and adv-apply.md, or only ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md since ops work spans all gates not just apply? adv-apply.md is apply-phase-scoped.
Packet anchors TASK_SCOPE/IN_SCOPE/DONE_WHEN were present; STOP_WHEN/VERIFICATION present. No packet defect.

subagent_reports.topic: Design leverage scout for orchestrator ops delegation

sources.label: adv.md Context-Optimal Execution
sources.summary: Current orchestrator context section is prose-only and already includes context-shed criteria, making a small additive bullet the lowest-risk change.

sources.label: ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md Delegation Routing
sources.summary: Current task-level routing table and context-shed marker live here; new operational routing should be placed separately to avoid changing Step 4.5 semantics.

sources.label: adv-apply.md Delegation Routing and Verify-Burst Delegation
sources.summary: Apply command already contains task-level routing plus verify-burst guidance; design should guard against duplicating the new ops routing table here.

sources.label: advance-meta spec rq-contextShed02
sources.summary: Spec requires adv.md prose-only context-shed guidance, adv-apply P23 diff-scan step, and drift tests for context-shed surfaces.

sources.label: advance-meta docs rq-contextShed02
sources.summary: Human-readable spec mirrors rq-contextShed02 and names expected drift-test guarantees.

sources.label: Existing asset test helper patterns
sources.summary: Existing tests assert command/agent markdown tokens directly; new ops drift test can reuse same lightweight asset-test style.

sources.label: No existing rq-contextShed drift guard found in plugin tests
sources.summary: Search returned no plugin/src results for rq-contextShed01, supporting current finding that context-shed drift tests are missing.

sources.label: Plugin verification scripts
sources.summary: check runs schemas:check, typecheck, isolation/lockfile checks, lint, and format:check; tests run through vitest.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Draft design is directionally sound: it preserves the primary adv authority boundary, keeps task-level Step 4.5 unchanged, uses adv.md as concise prose guidance, and centralizes the operational routing table in ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md only. Main leverage points: make test section extraction heading-bounded; encode routing-row assertions as a small data fixture; add rq-contextShed02 missing guard in the same asset test; avoid spec version/update churn beyond repo convention; add explicit negative assertions that adv-apply retains Verify-Burst guidance but does not contain the new ops table label or GitHub CI/check-run/status ops row.

validation.notes: No contract compromise blocker found. Risk is drift-test brittleness or accidental semantic bleed into adv-apply task-level routing if tests only assert broad tokens instead of section-scoped positives and negatives.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Auto-adopt test factoring and negative-assertion candidates. Surface only spec version bump as conditional: bump advance-meta version/updated_at if this repo convention requires metadata updates for new requirements; otherwise avoid churn. Keep design scope unchanged: no runtime enforcement, no OMR/model routing, no adv-atc.md edit, no Step 4.5 semantic change.
follow_ups: TASK_SCOPE/IN_SCOPE/OUT_OF_SCOPE/DONE_WHEN/STOP_WHEN/VERIFICATION anchors were present in the packet; no identity-anchor defect.
When implementing, run targeted vitest for the new asset test and at least bin/oc-test smoke or pnpm run check per repo policy.

subagent_reports.topic: Design validation for orchestrator ops delegation instruction/spec/test contract

sources.label: advance-meta spec.json rq-contextShed01/02
sources.summary: rq-contextShed01 governs task-routing Step 4.5 in ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md+adv-apply.md; rq-contextShed02 mandates adv.md Context-Optimal Execution prose-only (no table pipes) + adv-apply P23 diff-scan. rq-contextShed02.3 already requires prose-only/no-pipe adv.md assertion. New rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01 sits cleanly adjacent; no contradiction.

sources.label: adv.md Context-Optimal Execution
sources.summary: Section is currently prose-bullet only (inline vs delegate, due-diligence, context-shed). No markdown table. Adding prose-only ops bullets here is consistent with existing form and rq-contextShed02.1.

sources.label: ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md Delegation Routing table
sources.summary: Existing table is task-level routing (Step 4.5 Context-Shed) with rq-contextShed01/02 anchors. A new, separately-labeled Orchestrator-Session Operational Routing table is a distinct surface; placing it only here matches the one-table-surface decision and avoids adv-apply duplication.

sources.label: docs/specs/advance-meta.md mirror
sources.summary: Human-readable spec mirror exists and tracks contextShed requirements. Design correctly requires mirroring rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01 here; omission would create spec/doc drift caught by manifest-doc-drift tests.

sources.label: Established assets/drift test patterns
sources.summary: Deterministic readFileSync + section/token assertions and no-pipe-style structural checks are the established pattern. A new orchestrator-ops-delegation-assets.test.ts fits this convention. Note: no existing test currently asserts adv.md no-pipe for rq-contextShed02.3 in a dedicated file, so the new test also strengthens an existing thinly-covered guard.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is additive and structurally sound. It cleanly separates two delegation layers: (1) existing task-level Context-Shed routing (rq-contextShed01/02, Step 4.5) left untouched, and (2) a new orchestrator-session operational routing surface (rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01). Placement choices are verified against current source: adv.md is prose-only today so adding prose ops bullets is consistent; ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md is the correct single table home; docs mirror exists and must be updated to avoid drift-test failures; assets/drift test conventions support the proposed deterministic-extraction test. Authority-boundary preservation (primary adv keeps gate/task/checkpoint/archive/signoff/drift/contract authority) aligns with existing Sub-Agent Policy roles in adv.md (general = verify-only, not code-writing).

validation.notes: No spec contradictions, no security/release blockers. Minor cautions below are correctness/robustness improvements, not conflicts.

subagent_reports.recommendation: VALIDATED with minor cautions. The design correctly solves all stated objectives and contradicts no existing spec-law. Two correctness hardening points: (a) the new asset test's adv.md no-pipe assertion must scope strictly to the Context-Optimal Execution heading region (deterministic heading-to-next-heading slice) so it does not collide with the adjacent Sub-Agent Policy tables in the same file, which legitimately contain pipes; (b) since 'general not code-writing catch-all' is a stated constraint with no runtime enforcement, the spec scenario and test should assert the ops table maps research/edit rows to adv-researcher/adv-engineer/adv-designer (not general) so the prose/table cannot silently drift general into a code-writing default. Recommend keeping the new ops-routing row tokens (GitHub CI/check-run/status -> general) as exact assertable tokens. No simpler viable alternative materially reduces scope: collapsing ops guidance into the existing Step 4.5 table was correctly rejected because it blurs task-routing and session-routing semantics that rq-contextShed01/02 keep distinct.
follow_ups: Asset test: scope adv.md no-pipe assertion to the Context-Optimal Execution heading slice only, since adjacent Sub-Agent Policy tables in the same file contain pipes (adv.md:271-306).
Strengthen spec scenario + test to assert ops table routes code-writing/edit rows away from general (constraint: general is not a code-writing catch-all; no runtime enforcement exists).
Ensure docs/specs/advance-meta.md mirror is updated in lockstep to avoid manifest-doc-drift test failures.

verification.evidence: Targeted asset/drift test passed: 1 file, 5 tests. schemas:check passed via `tsx scripts/generate-json-schemas.ts --check`. `git diff --check trunk...HEAD` emitted no whitespace errors. Static review confirmed scoped diff versus trunk touches only .adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json, docs/specs/advance-meta.md, .opencode/agents/adv.md, ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md, and plugin/src/orchestrator-ops-delegation-assets.test.ts; .opencode/agents/adv-atc.md and .opencode/command/adv-apply.md are not modified.

required_main_agent_actions: Proceed with acceptance/release flow; no reviewer remediation required.

verification.evidence: Path preflight OK for all affected files. Targeted asset tests passed: 1 file, 5 tests. Smoke passed: schemas:check, typecheck, test-isolation check, lockfile policy check, lint, format:check, plus 2 smoke test files / 39 tests. git diff --check produced no whitespace errors. Harden inspection covered release/deploy readiness, production readiness, documentation hygiene, cleanup readiness, and residual slop/maintainability.

required_main_agent_actions: Proceed with release/archive sign-off flow; no hardening remediation required.

consumer_warnings.message: Initial task-scoped submission with TASK harden-change was rejected because harden-change is not an existing ADV task anchor; resubmitted as change-scoped harden:release per tool guidance.

wisdom.content: When testing prose-only agent sections, scope the no-pipe/no-table assertion to the exact Markdown heading slice. Adjacent sections like `Sub-Agent Policy` legitimately contain tables, so file-wide checks false-fail.

proposal.approval_evidence: Problem statement confirmed: orchestrator-session-level operational work (recon, audit, GitHub CI/check investigation, verify bursts) runs outside the task graph on primary adv premium model; existing Step 4.5 context-shed routing is task-scoped only. Direction: additive operational-delegation best-practice as prose in adv.md + table on ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md/adv-apply.md + new spec rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01, honoring rq-contextShed02 prose-only constraint.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery findings recorded; user approved criteria/agreement. Decisions: origin confirmed; adv-atc deferred; operational routing table in ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md only. Contract minted with 22 items.

design.approval_evidence: Design recorded. Independent adv-researcher validator returned VALIDATED with minor cautions; cautions folded into design. No unresolved conflict, user-value tradeoff, or contract-compromise risk.

planning.approval_evidence: User approved prep vision/task graph via reply: `approve`. Last human checkpoint before autonomous execution complete.

execution.approval_evidence: All 3 tasks completed with checkpoints. Verification passed: targeted asset/drift test and bin/oc-test smoke. No adv-atc.md, adv-apply.md, runtime, or OMR/model-routing changes.

acceptance.approval_evidence: User accepted delivered work via reply: `accept`. Acceptance proof persisted before prompt: contract matrix 22/22 pass/respected, executive summary persisted and verified, reviewer verdict READY with 0 blockers/issues, preview URL not_applicable.

documents.proposal:
## Cross-Project Origin

This change was created as a follow-up from **opencode-model-routing**.

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Source project | opencode-model-routing |
| Source path | `/home/jon/dev/opencode-model-routing` |

> **Note:** The originating project should be consulted for context on why this change is needed.


## Proposal: Orchestrator Operational Delegation

Add a durable, spec-backed "operational delegation" best-practice that complements (does not replace) the existing task-level Context-Shed routing.

### Scope boundary (what stays vs sheds)

Primary `adv` KEEPS inline (authority, never delegated):
- gate transitions and `adv_gate_complete`
- task-graph mutation (add/update/cancel/checkpoint/reclassify)
- checkpoint / archive / sign-off authority
- scope-drift, contract-compromise, safety, release decisions
- user-facing synthesis at checkpoints and final reports

Primary `adv` SHEDS sooner (operational, context+cost isolation):
| Trigger | Worker |
|---|---|
| >5 file reads/searches expected | `explore` |
| repo structure / dependency map / same-pattern scan | `explore` or `adv-tron` |
| DB/log/status/usage audit | `general` |
| **GitHub CI / check-run / status investigation** | `general` |
| repeated verify/test bursts | `general` |
| code edits after task scope known | `adv-engineer` |
| frontend/component edits | `adv-designer` |
| docs/source research first-pass | `general`; `adv-researcher` when sourced architecture authority needed |

### Surface placement (respect existing spec law)
Advance deliberately keeps `adv.md` Context-Optimal Execution **prose-only** (`rq-contextShed02`; drift tests forbid table pipes there) and puts routing **tables** on `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` / `adv-apply.md`.

This proposal honors that split:
1. `adv.md` — add operational-delegation criteria as **prose bullets** (no table), including the CI/check trigger and the "don't run a second recon/shell/test/CI-check cycle on the orchestrator before delegating" rule.
2. `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` / `adv-apply.md` — add the trigger **table** above (tables are spec-allowed there), framed as orchestrator-session operational routing, distinct from the per-task Step 4.5 table.
3. `adv-atc.md` — mirror the same prose (shares `adv` behavior minus `question`).

### Spec + tests
- New requirement `rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01` in `advance-meta`: defines the authority boundary + operational triggers, with scenarios and drift assertions.
- Keep `rq-contextShed01` (task-level 4.5) and `rq-contextShed02` (prose-only + P23 diff-scan) intact; new requirement is additive and consistent.
- Drift tests: assert `adv.md` carries operational-delegation prose tokens (still no table pipes in that section), and the table-surfaces carry the operational trigger table including the GitHub-CI token.

### Explicitly out of scope
- OMR / model-routing config (local toolbox concern; downstream of this).
- Runtime enforcement — consistent with existing self-enforced sub-agent policy, this stays instruction/spec-level.
- Changing the task-level 4.5 routing semantics.

### Why this shape is the long-term-correct one
The local stopgap (a routing table pasted into `adv.md`) violates `rq-contextShed02` and the drift suite. The durable form is prose in `adv.md` + table on the table-surfaces + a governing spec requirement — exactly how Advance already models the task-level context-shed split.

---

## Discovery Findings

### Discovery Checklist

| Step | Result | Evidence / reason |
|---|---|---|
| Skill Discovery | PASS | Loaded `adv-agent-tool-contracts`; no pending skill frontmatter found; no new skill needed. |
| Prior Research Extension | PASS | Cited `docs/repo-improve-prep.md` and `docs/adv-run-test-prep.md`; new finding: operational delegation is prompt/spec authoring, not a build-vs-buy/runtime change. |
| Conflict & Related Work Scan | PASS | `adv_change_validate` passed with expected pre-prep warnings (`NO_TASKS`, `NO_DELTAS`); no active conflicting change found in active list. |
| Edge Case Investigation | PASS | Structural edge cases captured below. |
| Design Question Depth | PASS | Open design questions annotated below. |
| Draft Spec Delta Shapes | PASS | Draft `rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01` shape captured below. |
| P25 Related-Pattern Scan | PASS | `explore` scan found existing task-level context-shed, verify-burst, and scanner delegation patterns. |
| LBP Check | PASS | Additive prose/table/spec split matches existing ADV pattern and external coordinator/worker best practice. |

### Skills Considered

- `adv-agent-tool-contracts` — loaded; relevant because this change modifies ADV agent/command/spec/test contracts.
- `adv-opportunity-scout` — applied through `adv-researcher` discovery scout packet.
- `lgrep` — active local-code discovery policy; used exact text search for context-shed/spec surfaces.
- No new skill created; problem is ADV instruction/spec policy, not a missing external domain methodology.

### Extends

- `docs/repo-improve-prep.md` — prior evidence that orchestration overhead, full-context tool work, and performance/latency visibility matter in this repo. New finding: operational delegation is a lower-risk instruction/spec lever than runtime replacement; it reduces primary-context pollution without changing Temporal/tool runtime.
- `docs/adv-run-test-prep.md` — prior evidence that verify/test work can be noisy/resource-heavy and benefits from structured execution/worker isolation. New finding: repeated verify bursts should be treated as orchestrator-session operational work, not only per-task `adv_run_test` behavior.

### Conflict Scan

- Active changes: no direct active conflict with `addOrchestratorOpsDelegation`; `conformAdvanceCi` is active draft but targets CI conformance, not delegation instructions.
- Validation: passed. Warnings `NO_TASKS` and `NO_DELTAS` are expected before planning/spec deltas are recorded.
- Agenda: large backlog includes delegation/test/status follow-ups, but no blocker to this additive instruction/spec change.

### Current State

- `.opencode/agents/adv.md:254-261` has Context-Optimal Execution prose for task-level context-shed only; no operational delegation triggers.
- `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md:722-738` and `.opencode/command/adv-apply.md:409-438` contain task-level delegation routing tables with Step 4.5.
- `.opencode/command/adv-apply.md:440-480` already has verify-burst delegation to `general`; closest precedent for operational output isolation.
- `.opencode/agents/adv-atc.md` has no Context-Optimal Execution / Sub-Agent Policy mirror section; adding ATC coverage is net-new authoring.
- `delegation-defaults` covers workflow steps and delegated substeps, not orchestrator-session operational work.
- New discovery finding: `rq-contextShed02.3` drift tests are not currently implemented; this change must add them rather than rely on them.

### Edge Cases

- `adv.md` prose-only: adding a table under Context-Optimal Execution would violate `rq-contextShed02` intent.
- Table duplication: adding operational routing rows to task-level Step 4.5 table could blur task-level vs orchestrator-session routing.
- Authority leakage: workers must not own gates, task mutation, checkpoint/archive/signoff, scope drift, or contract compromise.
- Threshold confusion: `>5 reads/searches` is a tool-call/context threshold; `~5 files or ~50 lines` is task implementation scope. They must stay distinct.
- ATC behavior: ATC has `question: false`; mirrored prose must not introduce inline-prompt behavior.
- `general` scope creep: mapping must remain operational/verify/CI/log/status work, not a catch-all for ADV code-writing.

### Open Design Questions

1. **Instruction table placement**
   - Trust model: joint; user scope/maintainer preference.
   - Blast radius: duplicated table maintenance vs missing apply-phase discoverability.
   - Alternatives: both `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` + `adv-apply.md` (proposal default), only `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` with pointer from `adv-apply.md`, or split table + reference.

2. **ATC inclusion**
   - Trust model: joint; user decides whether async ROADMAP executor should inherit same ops-shed behavior now.
   - Blast radius: including ATC adds new section/test coverage; excluding leaves ATC doing costly ops work.
   - Alternatives: include now (proposal default), defer follow-up, or include only a pointer to shared ADV behavior.

3. **Worker mapping precision**
   - Trust model: agent-resolved unless user wants product policy override.
   - Blast radius: too broad `general` mapping could weaken specialist routing; too narrow mapping leaves primary doing ops work.
   - Alternatives: keep proposal mapping; reserve `adv-tron` for codebase reconnaissance/hotspots; keep ADV code-writing on `adv-engineer`/`adv-designer`.

### Draft Spec Deltas

- `rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01` — Orchestrator Operational Delegation
  - Given primary `adv` expects broad operational work (>5 reads/searches, repo/dependency/same-pattern scan, DB/log/status/usage audit, GitHub CI/check-run/status investigation, or repeated verify/test bursts), when the work does not require gate/task/signoff/scope authority, then ADV should delegate it to the mapped worker before a second primary recon/shell/test/CI-check cycle.
  - Given `adv.md` Context-Optimal Execution is updated, when drift tests inspect the section, then operational delegation appears as prose bullets with no table pipes.
  - Given `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` and selected command surfaces are updated, when drift tests inspect them, then they include a clearly labeled orchestrator-session operational routing table with a GitHub CI/check-run trigger.
  - Given a worker returns findings, when the orchestrator acts, then primary `adv` retains gate completion, task mutation, checkpoint/archive/signoff, scope drift, and contract-compromise decisions.

### Related Pattern Scan

Similar patterns found:
- Task-level context-shed: `advance-meta` `rq-contextShed01` / `rq-contextShed02`, `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`, and `adv-apply.md`.
- Verify-burst delegation: `.opencode/command/adv-apply.md:440-480` isolates noisy verification in `general`.
- Discovery scanner delegation: `adv-discover` P25 scan uses `explore` / `adv-researcher` while orchestration decisions stay inline.
- Opportunity Scout split-load: `adv-discover` keeps schema/routing/adoption decisions with orchestrator and delegates candidate discovery.
- Scanner fan-out (`adv-slop-scan`, `adv-harden`) reinforces single-level delegation and no nested workers.

### LBP Check

Likely direction is long-term best practice: deterministic primary orchestrator retains judgment/authority; limited-context workers perform retrieval, audit, CI/status investigation, and verify bursts. This matches existing ADV task-level context-shed law and external coordinator/worker patterns. Runtime enforcement is not needed for this change.

### Recommended Objectives

1. Add spec-law requirement for orchestrator-session operational delegation without changing task-level Step 4.5 semantics.
2. Preserve primary `adv` authority boundaries for ADV state, gates, signoffs, drift, and release decisions.
3. Add prose-only operational delegation guidance to `adv.md`; no table in that section.
4. Add clearly labeled operational routing table to approved table surfaces.
5. Add drift tests proving prose/table placement and the GitHub CI/check-run trigger.
6. Keep worker mapping specialist-bounded (`explore`/`adv-tron`/`general`/`adv-engineer`/`adv-designer`), not a generic `general` catch-all.

### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS

B1 MEDIUM Boundaries — Table surface choice needs confirmation.
  Evidence: `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` / `adv-apply.md`.
  Reason: proposal names both, but discovery found `adv-apply.md` is apply-phase scoped while ops routing spans all gates.

F1 LOW Functional Scope — ATC inclusion is explicit but net-new.
  Evidence: `adv-atc.md — mirror the same prose`.
  Reason: current `adv-atc.md` lacks context-shed section and forbids inline questions.

S1 MEDIUM Completion Signals — Formal AC not yet approved.
  Evidence: `(no agreement.md)`.
  Reason: proposal has test ideas but not approved pass/fail criteria.

M1 LOW Missing Information — Cross-project origin still needs user confirmation in discovery.
  Evidence: `Cross-Project Origin ... opencode-model-routing`.
  Reason: command lineage validation requires user confirmation before agreement.

Coverage: B:P F:C S:P M:P
documents.problemStatement:
## Problem

Primary `adv` orchestrator runs expensive reconnaissance, audit, CI-check, and verify loops *outside* the task graph, on its premium model, with full session context.

### Evidence (7-day local usage audit)
- Primary `adv` = ~82% of reported cost and dominant token volume (~4.9B tokens) vs all subagents combined.
- Within `adv` sessions: ~10,858 read/search calls, ~7,228 shell/test calls, ~846 data/query probes, ~538 research/docs calls — much of it bulk operational work, not gate/task-graph orchestration.
- Top sessions (`ADV operational health status`, `ADV status command output`, `Programmatic CI tool audit`, `completeFleetCutover`) were dominated by reads + shell/test + CI/status probing the orchestrator could shed.

### Root cause
Advance's delegation routing (`rq-contextShed01`, Step 4.5 Context-Shed Test) is **task-graph-scoped** — it routes individual apply-phase tasks. It does NOT govern **orchestrator-session-level operational work** that never becomes a task:
- broad codebase reconnaissance (>5 reads/searches, repo-structure / same-pattern scans)
- DB/log/status/usage audits
- **GitHub CI / check-run / status investigation**
- repeated verify/test bursts

Because these are not tasks, the 4.5 routing never fires, and they run on the expensive primary model with no context shedding. The orchestrator keeps doing long recon + shell/test + CI-check loops before/around delegation.

### Why this matters now
- Cost: premium-model token burn on work cheaper context-isolated workers (`explore`, `general`, `adv-tron`) could do.
- Context hygiene: long noisy operational output pollutes the orchestrator's decision context.
- It is an instruction/spec gap, not a runtime bug — the fix belongs in Advance's durable agent prompt + spec, not a local override.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Add an `advance-meta` spec requirement for orchestrator-session operational delegation.
2. Preserve primary `adv` authority over gate transitions, ADV state mutation, checkpoints, archive/sign-off, scope drift, contract compromise, safety, and release decisions.
3. Add prose-only operational delegation guidance to `.opencode/agents/adv.md`.
4. Add one clearly labeled operational-routing table to `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` only.
5. Add drift/asset tests that structurally enforce the prose/table split and GitHub CI/check-run/status trigger.
6. Keep task-level Step 4.5 Context-Shed semantics unchanged.
7. Leave `adv-atc.md` unchanged in this change.

## Success Criteria

1. Primary `adv` guidance preserves orchestration authority while shedding operational work before repeated expensive primary cycles.
2. New spec law distinguishes orchestrator-session operational delegation from task-level Step 4.5 delegation.
3. Drift tests catch prose/table placement regressions and missing GitHub CI/check-run routing.
4. `adv-atc` remains out of scope for this change.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `advance-meta` includes `rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01` with scenarios for authority boundary, shed triggers, no-second-cycle rule, and prose/table placement.
2. `.opencode/agents/adv.md` Context-Optimal Execution includes prose-only ops delegation guidance; no table pipes appear in that section.
3. `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` includes one clearly labeled orchestrator-session operational routing table with GitHub CI/check-run/status investigation mapped to `general`.
4. `.opencode/command/adv-apply.md` task-level Step 4.5 semantics remain unchanged and do not duplicate the ops table.
5. Tests fail if `adv.md` ops guidance becomes table-form, if `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` lacks the ops table/GitHub CI token, or if task-level Step 4.5 wording is weakened.
6. No `adv-atc.md` behavior/prose change ships in this change.
7. Verification includes targeted asset/drift tests plus relevant schema/spec checks.

## Constraints

1. `rq-contextShed02` remains intact: `adv.md` Context-Optimal Execution uses prose bullets, not routing tables.
2. Operational delegation is instruction/spec/test-level only; no runtime enforcement is in scope.
3. Worker mapping remains specialist-bounded; `general` is for operational audits, CI/status investigation, and verify bursts, not ADV code-writing.
4. Primary `adv` keeps all ADV state authority and user-facing synthesis.
5. No changes to OMR/model-routing config.
6. No changes to `adv-atc.md` in this change.

## Avoidances

1. Do not paste the operational routing table into `.opencode/agents/adv.md`.
2. Do not alter task-level Step 4.5 Context-Shed semantics.
3. Do not make `general` the default code-writing worker.
4. Do not add runtime delegation enforcement.
5. Do not modify `adv-atc.md`; defer ATC handling to a follow-up if needed.

## Preview Applicability

visual_surface: false

Rationale: this change affects agent instructions, specs, and tests; no browser-visible or UI output surface changes are expected.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

1. Origin confirmed: `opencode-model-routing` and the 7-day local usage audit are valid discovery context.
2. `adv-atc` operational-delegation prose is deferred; `adv-atc.md` remains unchanged.
3. Operational routing table lives in `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` only; `adv-apply.md` must not duplicate it.

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

1. Use existing ADV pattern: spec law + prose in `adv.md` + table on allowed instruction surface + drift tests.
2. Treat `>5 reads/searches` as an orchestrator-session context/tool threshold, distinct from task-level `~5 files or ~50 lines` Step 4.5 threshold.
3. Preserve deterministic orchestrator authority; delegated workers gather/verify/inspect, while primary `adv` decides and mutates ADV state.

## Deferred Questions

1. Whether `adv-atc` should receive comparable operational-delegation guidance in a future change.

## Sign-Off

Approved by user reply: `approve` after criteria checkpoint for `addOrchestratorOpsDelegation`.
documents.design:
# Design

## Architecture Overview

This change adds an instruction/spec/test contract for **orchestrator-session operational delegation**. It is additive to the existing task-level Context-Shed routing.

Two delegation layers remain distinct:

1. **Task-level routing** — existing Step 4.5 Context-Shed Test in `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` and `.opencode/command/adv-apply.md`; unchanged.
2. **Orchestrator-session operational routing** — new guidance for primary `adv` to shed broad reads/searches, audits, CI/status investigation, and repeated verify bursts before repeated expensive primary cycles; new table lives only in `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`.

Primary `adv` remains the authority boundary. Workers gather, inspect, verify, and report; primary `adv` owns all ADV state mutation and user-facing decisions.

## Key Decisions

1. **Spec-law home: `advance-meta`**
   - Add `rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01` near `rq-contextShed01` / `rq-contextShed02` in `.adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json`.
   - Mirror the requirement in `docs/specs/advance-meta.md` so human-readable spec docs stay aligned.
   - Bump `advance-meta` version if required by existing spec-edit convention.

2. **`adv.md` receives prose only**
   - Update `.opencode/agents/adv.md` `## Context-Optimal Execution` with prose bullets.
   - Mention operational triggers: `>5 reads/searches`, repo/dependency/same-pattern scans, DB/log/status/usage audits, GitHub CI/check-run/status investigation, repeated verify/test bursts, and known-scope code edits routed to specialist workers.
   - Include the no-second-cycle rule: do not run a second primary recon/shell/test/CI-check cycle before delegating when the work is operational and authority-free.
   - No Markdown table or `|` pipe characters in the extracted Context-Optimal Execution section.

3. **One table surface: `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`**
   - Add a clearly labeled `### Orchestrator-Session Operational Routing` table in `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` near Delegation Routing.
   - Label it as session-level operational routing, not task-level routing.
   - Do not duplicate this table in `.opencode/command/adv-apply.md`.

4. **`adv-apply.md` remains task-routing-only**
   - Preserve Step 4.5 wording and priority order.
   - Do not add GitHub CI/check-run ops rows to the task-level Delegation Routing table.
   - Existing verify-burst section can remain as a task/apply-specific precedent; no contract change needed there.

5. **`adv-atc.md` out of scope**
   - No edits to `.opencode/agents/adv-atc.md`.
   - Future ATC mirroring can be a separate change because ATC has different HITL behavior (`question: false`) and currently lacks the whole Context-Optimal Execution section.

## ADR Drafts

No ADR needed. The decision is additive prompt/spec/test policy, not hard-to-reverse infrastructure or surprising architecture.

## Implementation Strategy

1. Add `rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01` to `.adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json`.
   - Scenarios:
     - Authority boundary remains inline.
     - Operational triggers delegate to mapped workers.
     - No-second-cycle rule fires for repeated primary recon/shell/test/CI-check cycles.
     - `adv.md` prose-only placement.
     - `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` table placement and GitHub CI/check-run/status trigger.
     - `adv-apply.md` task-level Step 4.5 remains unchanged and no ops table duplication.
     - `adv-atc.md` unchanged.

2. Mirror the spec delta in `docs/specs/advance-meta.md`.

3. Update `.opencode/agents/adv.md` `Context-Optimal Execution` with concise prose bullets.

4. Update `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` with one ops-routing table.
   - Suggested rows:
     - `>5 file reads/searches expected` → `explore`
     - `repo structure / dependency map / same-pattern scan` → `explore` or `adv-tron`
     - `DB/log/status/usage audit` → `general`
     - `GitHub CI / check-run / status investigation` → `general`
     - `repeated verify/test bursts` → `general`
     - `code edits after task scope known` → `adv-engineer`; frontend/component edits → `adv-designer`
     - `docs/source research first-pass` → `general`; use `adv-researcher` when sourced architecture authority is needed
   - Add a note below the table: primary `adv` keeps gate/task/checkpoint/archive/signoff/drift/contract-compromise authority.
   - Add a structural test assertion that code-writing rows do **not** route to `general`.

5. Add focused asset/drift test, preferably `plugin/src/orchestrator-ops-delegation-assets.test.ts`.
   - Helper extracts `## Context-Optimal Execution` from `adv.md` from heading to next `##` heading. Do not check the whole file; `Sub-Agent Policy` legitimately contains tables.
   - Assertions:
     - Section includes operational delegation tokens: `operational`, `GitHub CI`, `check-run`, `second`, `reads/searches`, `general`.
     - Section does not include Markdown table rows or `|` pipe characters.
     - `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` contains `Orchestrator-Session Operational Routing` and a `GitHub CI / check-run / status investigation` row mapped to `general`.
     - `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` code-edit rows map to `adv-engineer` / `adv-designer`, not `general`.
     - `.opencode/command/adv-apply.md` contains Step 4.5 Context-Shed Test but does not contain `Orchestrator-Session Operational Routing`.
     - `.adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json` contains `rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01`.
     - Same test should add the missing `rq-contextShed02.3` guard for existing context-shed prose/table placement where practical.

6. Run targeted verification first, then broader checks:
   - `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/orchestrator-ops-delegation-assets.test.ts` from repo root, or equivalent plugin-targeted vitest if wrapper cannot target repo-root path.
   - `pnpm run schemas:check` if spec JSON schema output or spec docs are generated/checked by repo flow.
   - `pnpm run check` or `bin/oc-test smoke` before acceptance.

## LBP Analysis

This design follows the repo's existing durable-instruction pattern:

- Spec law owns durable behavior.
- `adv.md` carries compressed runtime prose for primary agent behavior.
- `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` carries more detailed operational tables.
- Asset/drift tests prevent prompt/spec drift.
- No runtime enforcement is introduced for a self-enforced delegation policy.

It is preferable to a local model-routing override because it fixes the source instruction/spec gap and preserves provider/model flexibility.

## Affected Components

- `.adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json`
- `docs/specs/advance-meta.md`
- `.opencode/agents/adv.md`
- `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`
- `plugin/src/orchestrator-ops-delegation-assets.test.ts` (new)
- Existing tests may be referenced but should not require broad rewrites:
  - `plugin/src/delegation-matrix.test.ts`
  - `plugin/src/adv-designer-assets.test.ts`
  - `plugin/src/handoff-footer-drift.test.ts`

Explicitly unaffected:

- `.opencode/agents/adv-atc.md`
- `.opencode/command/adv-apply.md` behavior/Step 4.5 semantics, except tests assert no ops table duplication there.
- Runtime Temporal/storage/tool code.
- OMR/model-routing config.

## Design-Derived Criteria

1. Context-Optimal Execution section extraction for tests must be deterministic and bounded by Markdown heading boundaries.
2. The `adv.md` no-table assertion must fail on any `|` pipe character inside the extracted section only.
3. The `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` ops table assertion must require both the label and the GitHub CI/check-run/status row mapped to `general`.
4. The `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` ops table assertion must require code-writing rows to route to `adv-engineer` / `adv-designer`, not `general`.
5. The `adv-apply.md` guard must require Step 4.5 remains present and reject the ops table label.
6. Spec delta must include at least one scenario for each approved AC class: authority boundary, trigger mapping, no-second-cycle, prose/table placement, no `adv-atc` change.

## Risks / Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Ops table blurs with task-level Step 4.5 | Use a separate heading and wording: “orchestrator-session operational routing”; test `adv-apply.md` does not contain that label. |
| `adv.md` accidentally becomes table-form | Test extracted Context-Optimal Execution section for zero `|` pipe characters; do not check whole file because later sections contain legitimate tables. |
| `general` becomes catch-all code worker | Spec and tests say `general` is for audit/CI/status/verify operational work; code-writing rows must route to `adv-engineer` / `adv-designer`. |
| Missing mirror in generated docs | Planning tasks must update both `.adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json` and `docs/specs/advance-meta.md`, then verify spec checks. |
| `adv-atc` mismatch remains | Accepted as explicit out-of-scope and deferred question; no file edit in this change. |

## Design Leverage Scout

Scout: inconclusive — mandatory `adv-researcher` scout was attempted, but returned an empty result. No blocking issue; design proceeds per command degradation rule.

Auto-adopted candidates: 0.
Surfaced candidates: 0.

## Validator Result

Validator: `VALIDATED` with minor cautions.

Findings folded into design:

1. Scope the `adv.md` no-pipe assertion to `## Context-Optimal Execution` only; adjacent `Sub-Agent Policy` contains legitimate Markdown tables.
2. Add structural guard that operational code-writing rows route away from `general` and toward `adv-engineer` / `adv-designer`.
3. Mirror `advance-meta` spec delta into `docs/specs/advance-meta.md` in lockstep.

No unresolved `CONFLICT`. No contract-compromise risk identified.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

## Outcome

Delivered orchestrator-session operational delegation guidance for ADV. Primary `adv` now has durable instruction/spec/test coverage to shed authority-free operational work before repeated expensive primary cycles while preserving gate, task, checkpoint, archive, drift, contract, release, and user-facing decision authority.

## Verdict

APPROVED

## What Was Built

1. Added a red/green asset test for orchestrator operational delegation placement, routing, and out-of-scope guards.
2. Added `rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01` to `advance-meta`, mirrored it in human-readable spec docs, and bumped spec metadata.
3. Added prose-only operational delegation guidance to `.opencode/agents/adv.md`.
4. Added the single `Orchestrator-Session Operational Routing` table to `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`.
5. Verified `.opencode/agents/adv-atc.md`, `.opencode/command/adv-apply.md`, runtime code, and OMR/model-routing config stayed out of scope.

## What Was Verified

- Verdict: READY / APPROVED with 0 blocker findings and 0 issue findings.
- Tests: `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/orchestrator-ops-delegation-assets.test.ts` passed with 5 tests; `bin/oc-test smoke` passed after schemas:check, typecheck, lint, format:check, and 39 smoke tests.
- Preview URL: not_applicable — `visual_surface: false`; change affects instructions, specs, and tests only.
- Contract matrix: 22 required rows passed/respected; 0 failed, violated, or unknown.

## Remaining Concerns

None for this change. Future optional follow-up: decide whether `adv-atc` should receive comparable operational-delegation guidance in a separate ATC-scoped change.
documents.acceptance:
# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-06T18:36:48-04:00

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SC1 | success_criterion | Primary `adv` guidance preserves orchestration authority while shedding operational work before repeated expensive primary cycles. | pass | adv-reviewer verdict READY; `adv.md` now says primary adv delegates authority-free ops work before noisy/repeated cycles and resumes inline for synthesis/ADV state mutation. |
| SC2 | success_criterion | New spec law distinguishes orchestrator-session operational delegation from task-level Step 4.5 delegation. | pass | `rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01` added separately from existing `rq-contextShed01`/`rq-contextShed02`; `adv-apply.md` Step 4.5 unchanged by diff/static review. |
| SC3 | success_criterion | Drift tests catch prose/table placement regressions and missing GitHub CI/check-run routing. | pass | `plugin/src/orchestrator-ops-delegation-assets.test.ts` asserts prose/table placement and GitHub CI/check-run/status routing; targeted test passed 5/5. |
| SC4 | success_criterion | `adv-atc` remains out of scope for this change. | pass | Static diff and reviewer report: `.opencode/agents/adv-atc.md` unchanged; test asserts no ops-delegation content in adv-atc. |
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | `advance-meta` includes `rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01` with scenarios for authority boundary, shed triggers, no-second-cycle rule, and prose/table placement. | pass | `rq-orchestratorOpsDelegation01` added to `.adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json` with scenarios .1-.6; targeted asset test passed. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | `.opencode/agents/adv.md` Context-Optimal Execution includes prose-only ops delegation guidance; no table pipes appear in that section. | pass | Targeted asset test extracts `adv.md` Context-Optimal Execution, checks operational tokens, and rejects pipe characters/table rows; test passed. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` includes one clearly labeled orchestrator-session operational routing table with GitHub CI/check-run/status investigation mapped to `general`. | pass | Targeted asset test requires `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` label `Orchestrator-Session Operational Routing` and `GitHub CI / check-run / status investigation | general`; test passed. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | `.opencode/command/adv-apply.md` task-level Step 4.5 semantics remain unchanged and do not duplicate the ops table. | pass | Targeted asset test requires Step 4.5 text in `adv-apply.md` and rejects ops-table label/GitHub row there; reviewer static check says adv-apply unchanged. |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | Tests fail if `adv.md` ops guidance becomes table-form, if `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` lacks the ops table/GitHub CI token, or if task-level Step 4.5 wording is weakened. | pass | RED test failed before implementation on missing adv.md ops prose, ADV_INSTRUCTIONS ops table, and spec requirement; GREEN/VERIFY passed after implementation. |
| AC6 | acceptance_criterion | No `adv-atc.md` behavior/prose change ships in this change. | pass | Targeted asset test asserts `adv-atc.md` lacks ops-delegation/table tokens; static diff shows adv-atc unchanged; test passed. |
| AC7 | acceptance_criterion | Verification includes targeted asset/drift tests plus relevant schema/spec checks. | pass | Verification passed: `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/orchestrator-ops-delegation-assets.test.ts` exit 0, `bin/oc-test smoke` exit 0 including schemas:check, typecheck, lint, format:check, and 39 smoke tests. |
| C1 | constraint | `rq-contextShed02` remains intact: `adv.md` Context-Optimal Execution uses prose bullets, not routing tables. | respected | `adv.md` ops guidance is prose bullets; asset test rejects any pipe in Context-Optimal Execution; no ops table pasted into adv.md. |
| C2 | constraint | Operational delegation is instruction/spec/test-level only; no runtime enforcement is in scope. | respected | Only specs/docs/agent instruction/test files changed; no runtime enforcement code changed. |
| C3 | constraint | Worker mapping remains specialist-bounded; `general` is for operational audits, CI/status investigation, and verify bursts, not ADV code-writing. | respected | `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` maps audit/CI/status/verify to `general`; code-edit rows map to `adv-engineer` / `adv-designer`; asset test rejects code rows mapped to general. |
| C4 | constraint | Primary `adv` keeps all ADV state authority and user-facing synthesis. | respected | Spec and adv.md prose state primary adv retains gate/task/checkpoint/archive/signoff/drift/contract/release/user synthesis authority. |
| C5 | constraint | No changes to OMR/model-routing config. | respected | Static diff contains no OMR/model-routing config files; changed files limited to advance-meta spec/docs, adv.md, ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md, and asset test. |
| C6 | constraint | No changes to `adv-atc.md` in this change. | respected | Static diff and test confirm `.opencode/agents/adv-atc.md` unchanged. |
| DONT1 | avoidance | Do not paste the operational routing table into `.opencode/agents/adv.md`. | respected | `adv.md` Context-Optimal Execution contains no table pipes; operational table exists only in `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`. |
| DONT2 | avoidance | Do not alter task-level Step 4.5 Context-Shed semantics. | respected | `adv-apply.md` not modified in final diff; asset test checks Step 4.5 remains and no ops table duplicate appears. |
| DONT3 | avoidance | Do not make `general` the default code-writing worker. | respected | Asset test requires code edits route to `adv-engineer` and frontend/component edits to `adv-designer`, not `general`. |
| DONT4 | avoidance | Do not add runtime delegation enforcement. | respected | No runtime Temporal/storage/tool code changed; spec states runtime enforcement is not required. |
| DONT5 | avoidance | Do not modify `adv-atc.md`; defer ATC handling to a follow-up if needed. | respected | `.opencode/agents/adv-atc.md` unchanged; asset test asserts no operational delegation tokens there. |

clarify_findings.message: Proposal has no Scope section. What files and modules will be affected?
Proposal describes behavior that could fail but doesn't mention error handling or rollback. What happens when things go wrong?
